title: atalantia littoralis
intro: Atalantia littoralis, also known as the mangrove fruit or coastal atalantia, is a species of plant in the family Rutaceae, commonly found in tropical and subtropical regions. Traditionally, the plant has been used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Atalantia littoralis are attributed to its ability to aid in digestion, reduce fever, and alleviate respiratory issues such as asthma and bronchitis. In herbal preparations, the plant is typically used in the form of tea, infusions, or decoctions, which can be consumed to harness its medicinal properties.
